steps.title: Pin down the setup
steps.content: Coastal Western already controlled 85% of California's railways. The Sunset Route added the first transcontinental line across the southern US, ending in Southern California. So the blank describes something Coastal Western already had in rail access to the Pacific coast and then strengthened ("further solidified").

steps.title: Choose the word for already-strong control being reinforced
steps.content: We need a noun meaning controlling power or supremacy — an existing position of strength that the new route reinforces.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — dominance: means controlling power — fits with already controlling 85% and then strengthening that position. ✓
  • Choice B — creativity: describes inventiveness, not market control — nothing in the passage is about innovation. ✗
  • Choice C — insignificance: directly contradicts the 85% control figure — you cannot "solidify" insignificance through a major new route. ✗
  • Choice D — neutrality: implies a balanced, non-controlling stance — contradicts the controlling-share evidence. ✗
That is the correct answer.
